text-courier.simple_explanation: This message claims to be from Pakistan Post but uses a suspicious URL and creates urgency to pay a fee for a non-existent service.
red_flags: Suspicious URL domain (pakpost-delivery.xyz does not match official pakpost.gov.pk)
Urgency tactic (parcel will be destroyed)
Request for payment via unverified link
Incomplete address claim without specific details
safe_next_steps: Do not click the link or provide payment information.
Contact Pakistan Post directly using their official website or phone number to verify the parcel status.
Check your official correspondence or tracking number from Pakistan Post.
text-courier.reply_draft: This message appears suspicious. Please do not click the link or pay. Contact Pakistan Post directly using their official website or phone number to verify your parcel status.

text-fbr.simple_explanation: This message claims to be from the FBR but asks for sensitive personal and financial details (CNIC and bank card) via a link, which is a common phishing tactic.
red_flags: Claims to be from FBR without official verification
Asks for bank card details
Asks for CNIC number
Uses urgency ('today')
Provides a link to submit data
safe_next_steps: Do not click the link.
Do not share CNIC or bank card details.
Visit the official FBR website directly by typing the URL into your browser.
Contact the FBR helpline using a known official number if you have questions.

text-bank.simple_explanation: This message claims to be from HBL but asks for an OTP, which is a common scam tactic to steal account access.
red_flags: Asks for OTP
Urgency to share sensitive code
Generic security threat without specific account details
safe_next_steps: Do not share the OTP with anyone.
Contact HBL directly through their official website or app to verify the status.
Report the message to your mobile carrier.
text-bank.reply_draft: Hello, I received a message claiming my HBL account is suspended and asked me to share an OTP. I did not send the code. I will check my account status on the official HBL website and report this message.

image-courier.simple_explanation: This message claims a package delivery failed and includes a shortened URL, which is a common tactic in delivery-related scams.
red_flags: Shortened URL (cut.ly)
Urgent tone ('cannot be delivered', 'will be returned')
Unusual sender name ('Leopards')
Generic placeholder profile picture
safe_next_steps: Do not click the link.
Check your actual courier account or app for delivery status.
Contact your courier company directly using official contact details.
Ignore the message if you did not order anything.

image-mobile.simple_explanation: This message shows signs of a potential advance-fee or fake sale scam. It uses urgency, vague identity, and asks for contact outside the platform.
red_flags: Sender name is unusual (Hassan Roger's)
Asks to move conversation off-platform to WhatsApp
Provides a phone number that may not be valid
Price is unusually high for a second-hand phone
Date in message is in the future (Apr 17, 2026)
safe_next_steps: Do not share personal or financial information
Do not click any links or download files
Do not reply to the message
Block the sender if possible
Report the message to the platform (WhatsApp or Facebook Messenger)
Verify the seller independently through trusted channels

image-traffic.simple_explanation: This message claims to be from EasyPaisa about a traffic fine but uses a suspicious short link and urgent language. Traffic fines are typically issued by the Traffic Police, not EasyPaisa.
red_flags: Sender claims to be EasyPaisa but traffic fines are not handled by them
Uses a suspicious short URL (v1.mk/OmZIKUB)
Urgent tone: 'pay now to avoid additional penalties'
No official domain or contact details provided
safe_next_steps: Do not click the link
Check your traffic fine status via the official Traffic Police website or app
Contact the Traffic Police directly using verified official channels
